issues.title: Boolean state variable missing `is/has/can/should` prefix
issues.description: The boolean state variable `added` does not follow the boolean naming convention. Boolean variables should have an `is`, `has`, `can`, or `should` prefix to clearly communicate their type and intent. It is also exposed on the public hook return type `UseAddToCartReturn` as `added: boolean`.
issues.fix: Rename `added` to `isAdded` (state, setter, and return type interface field).

issues.title: Boolean state variable missing `is/has/can/should` prefix
issues.description: The boolean state variable `showInput` does not follow the boolean naming convention. Boolean variables should have an `is`, `has`, `can`, or `should` prefix.

issues.title: Context file located in application root instead of application/context subfolder
issues.description: CartContext.tsx and FlyToCartContext.tsx live directly under `features/cart/application/` rather than a dedicated `application/context/` subfolder, which is the pattern used in all other apps (`shared/application/context/ErrorContext.tsx`). This is inconsistent with the project pattern and makes the application layer root directory harder to navigate.
issues.fix: Consider moving to `apps/store/src/features/cart/application/context/CartContext.tsx` for consistency with the project's established folder pattern.

patterns.description: All 51 hook files across all apps correctly follow the `use` prefix convention without exception.
All component files in presentation/components and presentation/pages directories use PascalCase naming consistently.
All exported global constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE (e.g., PRODUCTS_QUERY_KEY, PERMISSION_GROUPS, USER_TABLE_COLUMN_COUNT).
Prop interfaces consistently use `ComponentName + Props` suffix across all apps.
Utility/infrastructure files correctly use camelCase (e.g., delegateQueries.ts, productMutations.ts, cartCookiePersistence.ts).
Type-specific files (GalleryTypes.ts, PaymentMethodTypes.ts, CategoryTypes.ts, SectionItemTypes.ts) correctly use PascalCase per the `UserTypes.ts` convention.
